message:
feat(workflows/runner): tick 183 fail_execution cx-threaded across 7 step-loop call sites (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Sixth tick of the workflows/runner.rs strangler-fig. Migrates
the most-called lifecycle helper — fail_execution runs on every
error, abort, and cancel path inside run_workflow_inner.

New helpers on WorkflowRunner (same pattern as tick 182):
- `fail_execution_with_cx` (leaf) — all 3 storage calls
  (get_workflow + upsert_workflow + record_usage_metric) route
  through their cx-first siblings. Preserves the "metric-write
  failure is warn-only" contract — the metric write uses
  `if let Err` on the cx-first path too, so a cancelled metric
  write logs-and-continues rather than aborting the failure
  cleanup.
- `fail_execution_maybe_cx` (dispatcher) — takes `Option<&Cx>`.

Call-site migrations in `run_workflow_inner`:
- L588 (plan-validation error)
- L646 (tick 181 per-step cancel seam; already inside a
  `Some(cx)` arm so calls `fail_execution_with_cx` directly,
  not via the dispatcher — the dispatcher would add a pointless
  Option<&Cx> wrap)
- L1032 (step-execute error branch)
- L1085 (retry-exhausted failure)
- L1395, L1468, L1526 (3 abort-reason branches in different
  step-kind error handlers)

All 7 sites now route through cx-aware storage when `cx` is
`Some`. Legacy path unchanged (`None` → legacy fail_execution).

Legacy `fail_execution` (L2109) and its single caller inside
the dispatcher fallback (L2235) preserved for the ambient-cx
path and for callers who haven't been migrated yet.

Verified via fork bypass: 31/31 workflows::runner:: tests pass.

Strangler-fig progress:
- ✅ Tick 178: entry shim
- ✅ Tick 179: startup-helper siblings (engine.rs)
- ✅ Tick 180: startup handshake (4 sites)
- ✅ Tick 181: per-step cancel seam
- ✅ Tick 182: update_execution_step (4 sites)
- ✅ Tick 183: fail_execution (7 sites) — this
- Tick 184+: mark_trigger_event_handled (2 sites) +
  complete_execution (1 site) + set_execution_waiting (2 sites)
- Tick 185+: migrate the 2 legacy callers (run + resume_incomplete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
